name: Chowing's Tavern Brunswick Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 Each Stewing Hen (6 Lbs) Or 2 Broilers (6 Lbs total)
2 Medium Potatoes, halved and diced to about 1/2 inch
4 Cups Corn from Cob or 2 (10 oz) Cans
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into pieces and simmer in 3 quarts of water until it can be easily removed from bones, about 2 hours.
2. Remove the chicken from the broth and add the raw vegetables. Simmer uncovered until the beans and potatoes are tender, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching.
3. Add the cooked chicken (boned and diced) and seasonings. Mix well.
4. Allow the stew to cool to room temperature before covering and refrigerating overnight.
5. The next day, reheat the stew over moderately-low heat until it reaches a slow simmer. Do not boil.
6. Serve with warm biscuits or bread.
Notes:
- If canned vegetables are used, drain them and include the juices in the chicken cooking liquid.
- Brunswick stew benefits from long, slow cooking.
- In some households, it is a rule that the stew cannot be eaten on the day it is made; the flavor will always improve with age.
